analytics.title: Analytics & Simulations

support.title: Support
support.subtitle: Find answers or get in touch
support.not_found: Didn't find what you were looking for?
support.faq_a1: The recommended flow is: (1) create your accounts under Accounts — bank, wallet, credit card; (2) import or enter your transactions under Transactions; (3) organize spending categories under Categories; (4) define your goals under Goals. After that the Dashboard already shows a complete picture of your financial health.
support.faq_q2: How do I add a bank account?
support.faq_a2: Go to Accounts → New Account. Choose the type (Checking, Savings, Credit Card, Investment, etc.), enter the initial balance, and save. All transactions entered for that account are automatically factored into the balance calculation.
support.faq_q3: How do I import a CSV statement?
support.faq_a3: Go to Transactions → Import CSV. The app accepts statements in the standard format used by the main Brazilian banks. On the import screen you can review and categorize each row before confirming.
support.faq_q4: How does the credit card invoice work?
support.faq_a4: Create an account of type "Credit Card". Purchases entered for it are associated with that month's invoice. Under Invoices you can track spending for the period, close the invoice, and record the payment — clearing the outstanding balance.
support.faq_q5: How do I organize my spending with categories?
support.faq_a5: Under Categories you create and customize categories (Food, Transport, Entertainment…) with icon and color. When recording a transaction, assign the matching category. The Dashboard and Analytics then show exactly where your money is going.
support.faq_q6: How do I set a financial goal?
support.faq_a6: Go to Goals → New Goal. Enter the name, target amount, and desired date. The app calculates progress based on the balance of accounts linked to the goal — ideal for an emergency fund, a trip, or any objective.
support.faq_q7: How do I record a transfer between accounts?
support.faq_a7: Under Transactions, use the "Transfer" button. Select the source and destination accounts — the app automatically creates an outflow from the source and an inflow to the destination without duplicating in the income/expense report.
support.faq_q8: Why is my balance different from what I expect?
support.faq_a8: The balance is calculated in real time by adding the account's initial balance to all recorded transactions. Check that the initial balance is correct under Accounts → Edit, and that there are no duplicate transactions or transactions with a future date not yet falling in the viewed period.
support.faq_a9: Under Recurrences you record fixed expenses or income (rent, subscriptions, salary) with a frequency and due date. The app generates installments automatically — you confirm, edit, or skip each occurrence as it comes due.
support.faq_q10: How does compound interest work in the Simulator?
support.faq_a10: The Investment Simulator projects your net worth month by month with an initial deposit plus monthly contributions, applying the annual rate converted to monthly. The chart shows invested capital vs. accumulated interest — and marks the year when interest exceeds capital.
support.faq_q11: What does the Inflation Simulator show?
support.faq_a11: It compares two curves over time: how much you would need to maintain your current purchasing power (adjusted for inflation) and how much your money would be worth if invested at a return rate. If the return curve stays above the inflation curve, your investment is preserving (and growing) your purchasing power.

settings.about_description: VindexValor is a smart personal finance management web app, developed as a Final Course Project (TCC) for the Post-Graduate Full Stack Development program at PUC-RS.
settings.about_open_source: The project is open source — contributions, suggestions, and feedback are welcome via GitHub.

landing.footer_copyright: © {{year}} VindexValor. TCC PUC-RS. All rights reserved.
landing.meta_description: Take control of your finances with ease, track investments, and reach your goals with VindexValor.
landing.hero_subtitle: Smart Personal Finance Management. Take control of your money, track investments, and reach your goals.
landing.about_p1: VindexValor is a smart personal finance management web app, developed as a Capstone Project (TCC) for the Full Stack Development Postgraduate program at PUC-RS.
landing.about_p2: The goal is to make financial control easier, offering intuitive tools for expense management, inflation (IPCA) projections, goals, and detailed tracking of your net worth over time.
landing.about_p3: The project is open source — contributions and feedback are welcome.
landing.features_subtitle: Everything you need for a healthier, more predictable financial life, all in one platform.
landing.feature_accounts_desc: Create and manage multiple accounts (Checking, Savings, Credit Card) with a unified view of balances and limits.
landing.feature_transactions_desc: Full control over Income and Expenses, transfers between accounts, and flexible categorization.
landing.feature_recurring_desc: Manage fixed bills, installments, and salaries with the smart automatic recurrence engine.
landing.feature_analysis_desc: Visualize your spending habits by category with interactive charts and budget limits.
landing.feature_investments_desc: Record returns, investment types, and simulate your wealth growth based on historical rates.
landing.feature_goals_desc: Set and track goals like 'Buy a Car' or 'Emergency Fund', linking real balances to your dreams.
landing.tech_subtitle: Built with modern technologies to ensure performance, security, and the best user experience.
landing.cta_final_title: Ready to take control of your finances?
landing.cta_final_subtitle: Create your account and get a simple, modern, and secure way to track your finances. Organize accounts, log transactions, and view your spending all in one place.

cookie.modal_desc: We use cookies to improve your experience. Customize your preferences below according to the intended purpose.
cookie.essential_desc: These cookies are necessary for the site to function, such as user authentication and security. They cannot be disabled.
cookie.preferences_desc: Allow the site to remember choices you make (such as your language or region) and provide enhanced, more personalized features.
cookie.analytics_desc: Help us understand how visitors interact with the site by collecting and reporting information anonymously.
cookie.marketing_desc: Used to track visitors across websites. The intention is to display ads that are relevant and engaging for the individual user.

account_types.title: Account Type Mapping
account_types.subtitle: Reference the relationship between account types and their subtypes in the system.
account_types.info_text: The account subtype (account_subtype) is used internally to apply specific rules, such as hiding the "Initial Balance" field and showing "Card Limit" for Credit Card accounts.
account_types.credit_card_desc: Account used to manage credit card spending, invoices, and limits.
account_types.checking_desc: Standard checking account for daily transactions, payments, and receipts.
account_types.savings_desc: Savings account to store money long-term with low risk.
account_types.investment_desc: Account for managing investments at brokerages.
account_types.crypto_desc: Account for digital assets and cryptocurrencies (e.g. BTC, ETH).
account_types.other_desc: Other financial account types not categorized above.
